manifesto.narrative:
[DRAFT — Profile incomplete. Awaiting full questionnaire responses.]

I approach politics through an effective altruist lens: what interventions produce the most good per dollar or hour invested? This makes me instinctively skeptical of symbolic policy and drawn to evidence-backed programs with measurable outcomes.

I'm pro-natalist — I believe society should make it dramatically easier for people to have children if they want them, through better parental leave, childcare infrastructure, housing affordability, and healthcare access. This isn't a conservative position; it's a recognition that our current systems are failing young families.

In New York, housing is the existential issue. The city's inability to build enough housing is a policy failure that cascades into everything else — childcare costs, commute times, inequality, brain drain. I support City of Yes and any reform that makes it easier to build.

I lean technocratic. I'd rather have decisions made by people with domain expertise than by political consensus. I think most political discourse is an enormous waste of human capital, which is part of why I don't engage much with it — but I recognize that disengagement is itself a political choice with consequences.

key_descriptors: Low engagement appetite — high donation willingness
political_context.incomplete_note: Needs full questionnaire to refine values tradeoffs and issue positions. Current manifesto is a draft based on archetype assumptions.
